other_details:
This mutation was identified on one Southern Italy CF chromosome.
The mutation was identified by DHPLC screening of CFTR exons followed by sequencing of both the strands.
phenotype:
This mutation was identified in a CF patient from the Calabria region of Southern Italy. The individual (female, 28y) carrier W57G on the other allele.
The patient has pulmonary disease and positive sweat chloride.
This mutation was identified in trans of F508del in a fetus presenting an hyperechogenic bowel at 23 weeks of pregnancy. It was indeed transmitted from the mother.

other_details: This heterozygous change has been found in an Indian Patient with Chronic Pancreatitis.
phenotype: This patient (32 Years old Male) was diagnosed with Idiopathic Chronic Pancreatitis at the age of 30 yrs. As CFTR gene is also involved in Chronic Pancreatitis so this patient was screened for mutations of CFTR gene by SSCP. This patient was also having other CFTR  variant p V456A in exon 9. This patient was found to be negative for common mutation of SPINK1 and PRSS1 gene involved in pancreatitis.

This mutation was identified by sequencing in a newborn presenting positive IRT at birth (>110 ng/ml - IRT normal value < 65 ng/ml) and carrying p.Gly551Asp on the other allele. The sweat test was positive (98/100 mmol/l).
The family was originating from Morocco.
Spanish CF patient, female (8 months) presenting meconium ileus, positive sweat test and the genotype F508del / 876-2A>G

This mutation was identified by sequencing in a newborn presenting positive IRT at birth (104ng/ml - IRT normal value < 65 ng/ml) and carrying p.F508del on the other allele. The sweat test was borderline at birth (48/45 mmol/l).
At 5 yo., he is pancreatic sufficient and no symptoms of classical CF
This mutation was detected by DGGE analysis and  confirmed by Sequencing in a  35 years old Italian man  with oligoasthenospermia.
This probable polymorphism was identified by direct sequencing in a CBAVD patient who was compound heterozygous for 2 CFTR mutations.
This nucleotide change was identified in a spanish male with severe oligoasthenoteratozoospermia. He carries also IVS8-5T allele but we couldn´t stablish if  both variations are in cis or in trans.

reference: Are p.I148T, p.R74W and p.D1270N cystic fibrosis causing mutations? Claustres M, Altiéri JP, Guittard C, Templin C, Chevalier-Porst F, Des Georges M. BMC Med Genet. 2004 Aug 2;5:19.
phenotype:
This mutation was identified on an allele carrying p.Ile148Thr. The patient was diagnosed with severe CF and was compound heterozygous for this complex allele and a frequent CFTR mutation: [c.3263dupA;p.Ile148Thr]+[p.Arg334Trp]
Claustres et al., 2004
This probable polymorphism was identified by direct sequencing in a CBAVD patient originating from Maghreb who was compound heterozygous for Trp1282X and [TG11T5;Val562Ile]

other_details: Father had no CF symptoms
phenotype: This mutation was identified in Polish 26 year old man whose 4 day old child died because of Meconium Ileus. Mother of his child is carrier of the p.F508del  allele. No other mutation was found after sequencing of all 27 CFTR exons.
This probable polymorphism was identified by direct sequencing in a partner of a CBAVD patient.

Complete scanning allowed to identify this missense mutation and the variant 3849+40A>G in a CBAVD patient. Familial segragation was not available.
The sweat test values were borderline and the patient had bronchiectasis.

The proband is a 19 year old caucasian female with clinical symptoms of classic CF and sweat chlorides of 90 and 87 mmol/L.
The patient harbors a large deletion of exon 4-10 on one allele, and a novel 20 bp deletion in exon 7 on the second allele

other_details: The above mutation was identified by SSCP analysis and characterized by direct DNA sequencing.  The mutation was not found on 100 non-CF chromosomes.
phenotype: This was a known case of 3 month old baby operated for tracheo-esophageal fistula with esophageal atresia. He was presented with vomiting and cough since one month, rapid breathing and chest indrawing. He was pancreatic sufficient and sweat chloride levels were 63.8 mEq/L. Blood culture was positive for K. pneumonae

Czech female patient, diagnosed at 3.5 years of age, at 2 years rectal prolapse. Sweat chloride level 79,4...89,8 mmo/l,  PI, clubbing fingers, bronchiectasy, air trapping (on HRCT), colonized with Pseudomonasis aeruginosa. The other mutant allele is p.F508del

other_details: This deletion is different from the deletion of exon 19 previously described by Girodon et al. (1999 ). Breakpoints were characterized by relative quantification of introns 18 and 19 on Light Cycler and direct sequencing of junction fragments.
phenotype:
This large deletion was identified by semi-quantitative fluorescent PCR in a 6 years old girl who carries F508del in trans.
She presents a classical form of CF.

other_details: This duplication was identified in a CF patient’s mother originating from Greece by semi-quantitative fluorescent multiplex PCR. Breakpoints were characterized by relative quantification and direct sequencing of junction fragments.
phenotype: This duplication was identified in a CF patient’s mother originating from Greece. The patient is 20 yo and was diagnosed by neonatal screening. She carries the F508del on the other chromosome. Phenotypic data: positive sweat test (90 mEq/L), PS and mild pulmonary symptoms.

This patient (19Year/M) was diagnosed with Idiopathic Chronic Pancreatitis at the age of 9 yrs. As CFTR gene is also involved in Chronic Pancreatitis so this patient was screened for mutations of CFTR gene by SSCP. This patient was found negative for Common DeltaF508 and intron 19 mutation of CFTR gene and common p. R122H and p. N29I mutation of PRSS1 gene. This patient was also homozygous for p. N34S mutation of SPINK1 gene involved in Chronic Pancreatitis. This patient was also carrying other sequence variation c. 1812-99 C>T in of CFTR gene.

This missense mutation was identified by sequencing in a newborn presenting positive IRT at birth (>100 ng/ml - IRT normal value < 65 ng/ml), no common mutation (Elucigene kit CF30 / Tepnel) and positive sweat test (89/91mmol/l).
The baby is homozygous for this missense mutation, his parents are consanguineous and originating from Kurdistan.

other_details:
First patient: The above mutation was identified by SSCP analysis and characterized by direct DNA sequencing.  The mutation was not found on 100 non-CF chromosomes.
Another patient: This Heterozygous change has been found in an Indian Patient with Chronic Pancreatitis.
phenotype:
The chief complaint of this 13 yrs old female patient was recurrent cough for the last five years. Her sweat chloride level was 64 mEq/L; and percentage predicted FVC and FEV1 were 51% and 52% respectively. Chest X-ray showed bilateral middle zone infiltrates (pers. corr. Prasad).
Another patient: This patient (29Yr/M) was diagnosed with Chronic Pancreatitis at the age of 27 years. As CFTR gene is also involved in Chronic Pancreatitis, so this patient was screened for mutations of CFTR gene by SSCP. This patient was found negative for Common Delta F508 and Intron 19 mutations of CFTR gene. This patient was also found negative for common mutations of other genes (SPINK1, PRSS1) involved in Chronic Pancreatitis. This patient was also reported to have Sequence Variations (1540 A>G and 1525-61 A>G) of exon 10 and intron 9 of CFTR gene.

other_details: The above mutation was identified by SSCP analysis and characterized by direct DNA sequencing.  The mutation was not found on 100 non-CF chromosomes.
phenotype: This 4 month old female child presented with recurrent respiratory infections and loose stools since 2 months. Her sweat chloride level was 113 mEq/L, was PI, and chest x-ray showed pneumonia, consolidation and hyperinflated chest. Elder sib also had similar complaints and died of pneumonia (pers. corr. Prasad).

other_details: The above mutation was identified by SSCP analysis and characterized by direct DNA sequencing.  The mutation was not found on 100 non-CF chromosomes.
phenotype: He was 8 years old, PS, with mild cough and failure to thrive. Sweat chloride was 61 mEq/L. Chest x-ray showed hyperinflation (pers. corr. Prasad).

other_details: The above mutation was identified by SSCP analysis and characterized by direct DNA sequencing.  The mutation was not found on 100 non-CF chromosomes.
phenotype: This 8 years old male had breathing problem since 5 months of age. He was on treatment for bronchial asthma and had taken antitubercular treatment despite Montaoux and culture tests negative. He was PI, sweat chloride was 98 mEq/L and sputum culture was positive for P aeruginosa (pers. corr. Prasad).

other_details: This was a case of 34 years old male with PI, bronchiectasis and chronic cough. His sweat chloride was 108 mEq/L, IRT 0.83µg/L and CT abdomen showed pancreatic atrophy (pers. corr. Prasad).
phenotype: This was a case of 34 years old male with PI, bronchiectasis and chronic cough. His sweat chloride was 108 mEq/L, IRT 0.83µg/L and CT abdomen showed pancreatic atrophy (pers. corr. Prasad).

other_details: The above mutation was identified by SSCP analysis and characterized by direct DNA sequencing.  This mutation was detected in infertile male with congenital bilateral absence of vas deferens. Mutation on other allele was Delta F508 mutation. This mutation was not found on 100 non-CF chromosomes.
phenotype: This is a case of 32 year old infertile male married for 8 years. His sweat chloride was 88 mEq/L, Testosterone- 250ng/dl, FSH- 3.8 mIU/ml, LH- 4.3mIU/ml, Urea- 28 mg/dl and Creatinine- 0.9mg/dl. Semen analysis demonstrated nil sperm count and FNAC revealed normal spermatogenesis. Transrectal ultrasound and abdominal ultrasonography showed no other urogenital malformation. Chest X-ray was normal. However, on physical examination congenital bilateral absence of vas deferens was reported (pers. corr. Prasad).

other_details: The above mutation was identified by SSCP analysis and characterized by direct DNA sequencing.  This mutation was detected in infertile male with congenital bilateral absence of vas deferens. Mutation on other allele was not identified. This mutation was not found on 100 non-CF chromosomes.

phenotype: This is a case of 11 year old male child with a history of frequent episodes of cough and difficulty in breathing since 2 years of age, abdominal distension on and off, voluminous oily stools and failure to thrive. Spirometry revealed restrictive lung function (FVC and FEV1 were 61 and 64% respectively) (pers. corr. Prasad).

phenotype: This is a case of 28 year old infertile male married for 5 years. His sweat chloride was 90 mEq/L, Testosterone- 405ng/dl, FSH- 3.9 mIU/ml, LH- 4.0mIU/ml, Urea- 27 mg/dl and Creatinine- 0.8mg/dl. Semen analysis demonstrated nil sperm count and FNAC revealed normal spermatogenesis. Transrectal ultrasound and abdominal ultrasonography showed no other urogenital malformation. Chest X-ray showed hyperinflation. On physical examination, congenital bilateral absence of vas deferens was reported (pers. corr. Prasad).

other_details: Found in an 11 year-old girl who is heterozygous for F508del and whose brother is homozygous for F508del. She also carries the L467F and 3030G/A polymorphisms. The phase of all four sequence changes is unknown.Three separate splice site prediction programs predict a possible effect on splicing.NB: No clinical or genetic investigations have been carried out on the parents.

Missense mutation Arginine to Glycine at codon 1283. Predicted by PolyPhen as Probably Damaging; Align GVGD classifies it as C65 (most likely to interfere with function); highly conserved nucleotide and amino acid.
phenotype: The mutation was found in a patient, now 3 months old, who presented with meconium ileus. No other clinical information is known.

other_details: Detection by sequencing the CFTR gene
phenotype: Asymptomatic female. Heterozygositiy screening was performed since her partner is ΔF508 heterozygous. He was screened for CFTR mutations due to azoospermia

other_details: This mutation was identified on one Italian  CF chromosome. The above mutation was identified by DGGE analysis and confirmed by DNA sequencing.
phenotype: This mutation has been found in an adult ( 40y old),  Italian man  studied for couple’s infertility. He had a normal semen analysis.

other_details: This mutation was identified by DGGE analysis and confirmed by DNA Sequencing
phenotype: The above mutation was detected in an Italian man studied for couple’s infertility. He had  normal semen analysis.

other_details: This mutation was identified on 1 Italian CF patient, applying a protocol of extended mutational search (5’-flanking region, all the exons and adjacent intronic regions) by direct sequencing. No other mutations were found on the same allele. The 2789+5GtoA mutation was found on the other allele.
phenotype: The individual (male, 4 years) with 907delCins29 / 2789+5GtoA genotype was selected by neonatal screening and diagnosed CF. He is PS with sweat chloride test at 85 and 93 mmol/l. He has mild lung disease with intermittent P. aeruginosa isolates, peribronchial infiltration and moderate air trapping. No meconium ileus. No liver involvement.

other_details: This mutation was identified in two female siblings with CF.
phenotype: The sisters (ages 15 and 16) were diagnosed at ages 4 months and 1.5 months, respectively. The elder sister has a sweat chloride of 117 mmol/l and is taking pancreatic enzymes. She was born at 32 weeks gestation at a local hospital but was subsequently sent to a children’s hospital for “CF testing” on day of life 3, which corroborates the indication of meconium ileus in the US Cystic Fibrosis Registry. At age 4 she developed a Staphylococcus aureus infection and at age 7, Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Her most recent FEV1 was 2.68 L (90% predicted). She is 168 cm tall and weighs 63 kg. The younger sister has a sweat chloride of 101 mmol/l and is also on pancreatic enzymes. She was diagnosed with Staphylococcus aureus at age 6, Pseudomonas aeruginosa at age 11, and MRSA at age 14. Her most recent FEV1 was 2.83 L (86% predicted). She is 167 cm tall and weighs 55 kg. Both have mutation [delta]F508.

other_details: This mutation was identified on 1 Italian CF patient, applying a protocol of extended mutational search (5’-flanking region, all the exons and adjacent intronic regions) by direct sequencing. The F508del mutation was found on the other allele. The place of origin of the father, carrying the K442X mutation, is Sardegna.
phenotype: The individual (male, 2 years) with F508del / K442X genotype was selected by neonatal screening and diagnosed CF. He is PI with sweat chloride test at 80 and 76 mmol/l. He has mild lung disease with parenchymal infiltration without colonization. No meconium ileus. No liver involvement

other_details: detected during carrier screening in a healthy Turkish person
phenotype: healthy person

other_details: This mutation was identified by direct sequencing on one Chinese CF chromosome.

The ultrasound of abdomen showed liver cirrhosis and splenomegaly. The patient was also heterozygous for W679X mutation. Her patients had normal phenotype

other_details: The mutation was identified in an Iranian infertile man.
phenotype: The patient (male) was diagnosed at 31 years old with non-obstructive azoospermia. He carries A120T mutation on the other allele. Sperm retrieval failed in the patient using testicular sperm extraction (TESE).

other_details: This mutation was identified on one allele of CF patient from Ukraine (Carpathian Mountains region).  The other mutant allele is F508del.
phenotype: Female patient (2005 y.o.b), diagnosed at the age of 6 month. Recurrent bronchoobstructive disease since age of 2 month.Sweat chloride level 100,6 mmo/l,  PI, malabsorption syndrome.Cow's milk protein intolerance. Steatorrhea.Bronchiectasis, colonized with Staphylococcus aureus.  No one episode of Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Gastroesophagos reflex and enamel hypoplasia of all teeth.

other_details: This mutation was identified on one Brazilian CBAVD chromosome.
phenotype: The individual (a 30 year-old Caucasian man of Spanish descent), was referred to a pneumologist due to chronic respiratory infections, sinusitis and rhinitis since early childhood, who referred him to an urologist. Physical examination showed bilateral absence of vas deferens (CBAVD) and a cyst on the head of the right epididymus. Seminal analysis revealed azoospermia and low volume of ejaculate (<1mL) secondary to bilateral hypoplastic seminal vesicles identified by transretal ultrasound. The abdominal ultrasound showed normal topic kidneys and the sweat test showed normal chloride concentration.

This patient was heterozygous for common N34S mutation of SPINK1 gene involved in Chronic Pancreatitis. This patient was also having other reported sequence variations c. T/G 2694, c. 3041-92G>A, p. M470V, c.1525-61 A>G of CFTR gene.

The c.2752-1_2756delGGTGGCinsTTG mutation is a complex mutation, in which six nucleotides (GGTGCC)are deleted from 2752-1 (intron14a) until nucleotide 2756 (exon14b) and replaced by three nucleotides (TTG). phenotype: It changes an asparagine to a lysine at amino acid 900 of the protein. This mutation was found in a third generation Venezuelan female patient carrying the WT allele on the other chromosome. The patient has a confused phenotype with late onset of disease (11 years old), but has low body weight. She has had two border line sweat chloride tests (40 and 39 mmol/L).

It changes an asparagine to an aspartic acid at amino acid position 373 of the protein. This mutation was found in a third generation Venezuelan male patient carrying no mutation on the other chromosome. The patient has respiratory and gastric symptomatology that appeared for the first time at 2 months of age.  He had two border line  sweat chloride tests (55 and 45 mmol/L).

other_details: Because of the clinical findings, the p.Glu815* mutation was classified as a CF causing mutation.
phenotype: The p.Glu815* mutation was caused by the transversion of G to T at nucleotide position c.2444 in exon 13. It changes a glutamic acid to a stop codon at amino acid 815 of the protein. This mutation was found to be homozygotic in a third generation Venezuelan male patient. The patient has respiratory and gastric symptomatology that appeared for the first time at age 6 months, and he had two positive sweat chloride tests (111 and 108 mmol/L)

The p.Trp277* mutation was caused by the transition of G to A at nucleotide position c.830 in exon 6b. It changes a tryptophan to a stop codon at amino acid 277 of the protein. This mutation was found in a third generation Venezuelan male patient carrying the p.Phe508del mutation on the other chromosome. The patient has respiratory symptomatology that appeared for the first time at 3 months of age.  He had two positive sweat chloride tests (115 and 100 mmol/L).

other_details: The analysis of the complete sequencing of the CFTR gene revealed two different trans variants; c.3266G>A (p.Trp1089* or W1089*) previously reported as a CF-causing variation, and c.1762G>T, which had not been previously reported. The mother was carrier of p.Trp1089*. Since the father was not a carrier of any CF variant, paternity was tested and confirmed using 23 STR genetic markers (paternal probability >99.99 %), revealing that the p.Glu588* is a de novo variant.

The g.98818_98819del (NG_016465.4) variant was discovered in exon 11 of the CFTR gene, by Sanger sequencing, in which two thymine was deleted in heterozygous state. As a result, this deletion formed the premature termination codon UGA after replacing serine for leucine. In silico analysis in MutationTaster suggests that this deletion can alter functions of key regions such as the ATP-binding domain and the PDZ-binding domain. Furthermore, this premature termination codon promotes a loss of approximately 65% of full-length CFTR protein, resulting in nonsense-mediated mRNA decay (NMD).

phenotype:
He has been diagnosed at 3 months old, with lung infections. He carries F508del on the other allel. He has pancreatic insufficiency and taking pancreatic enzyme replasman treatment.
Abdominal ultrasound revealed liver parenchymal heterogenity and taking ursodeoxycholic acide. Pseudobartter syndrome history was negative. Nasal poliposis was positive He has osteopenia. He had history of ABPA, he has chronic P. aeruginosa colonization.  He has severe lung involvement with severe bronchiectasis, and needs continuous oxygen treatment.
He has recurrent hospitalizations due to pulmonary exacerbations. Lung function test: FEV1: %34  FVC: %59  FEV1/FVC: %48. Lung transplantation has been decided at his last visit. (Sweat test Cl:152 mEq/lt)

other_details: In August 21, Hannover's CF center was contacted by Dr. Raraigh who had identified another child of romanian descent with a typical clinical CF presentationi and the submitted mutation, making this mutation likely disease causing.
phenotype:
Index patient was diagnosed in Romania, of caucasian descent at age of two months due to failure to thrive and fatty stools via sweat testing. Genetics were performed via ECFS and revealed four hitherto unknown variants in addition to F508del in trans. Apart from the entered mutation, the additional mutations were:
c.1210-1638C>T; c.54-4458_54-445delAAC; c.3717+4941G>T. Child transferred to Hannover Medical University's CF center at age four years and has been cared for there since. Equivocal diagnosis was confirmed via sweat testing (chloride at 99 mmol/L, p-elastase <15µg/g (nv >200) and intestinal current measurement, providing evidence for classic CF with little residual function. Second and third throat swab yielded MRSA which was eradicated within the following year and Pseudomonas aeruginosa which remained chronic in spite of aggressive treatment. Lung functions show a degree of compromise above that seen in the larger Hannover cohort (spirometry FEV1-values 75-85%, N2LCI2.5 at 8-10 points, radiological CN-Score at age 6 5 points).

other_details:
CFTR functional assays:
sweat chloride at 97 mmol/L
Intestinal current measurement performed 2017 at Hannover Medical School according to ECFS SOP:
sum values (carbachol, forskolin/IBMX/DIDS-insensitive histamine) in four biopsies: 7, -6, 11, -6 µA/cm*2 confirming CF-typical pattern with little CFTR residual function
CFTR sequencing initially revealed three SNPs (in addition to F508del), however another patient with N1303K and this mutation was discovered 3 years later, leading to identification of this mutation as causative of the phenotype
phenotype: Typical presentation with failure to thrive within first year of life, pancreatic insufficiency, chronic PsA colonization detected at age four.

other_details: Per genetics report: This is not a previously reported CFTR mutation, but predicted to be deleterious by bioinformatic analysis. A different nucleotide substitution has been reported at this position.
reference: Genetic report from CFF MAP Program/John's Hopkins DNA Diagnostic Lab dated 7/30/2013
phenotype: Caucasian female diagnosed at age 14 due to recurrent bronchitis and exercise intolerance. Also has history of sinus disease requiring surgery. Sweat test 90/89. Chronically grows only MSSA on airway cultures. Pancreatic sufficient. Mild lung disease.

other_details: Lab report from Sonora Quest Laboratories dated 4/15/2009 states: The novel CFTR gene variant detected is R1479K. It is caused by a G>A change at nucleotide 4568 in exon 24 of the CFTR gene. This single nucleotide substitution led to the replacement of an arginine by lysine at position 1479 of the translated CFTR protein. R1479 is not a well conserved amino acid among CFTR orthologs. Nevertheless, analysis of the R1479K change using PolyPhen, an online tool for prediction of the effect of amino acid changes in protein structure and function, indicated that the R1479K change is "possibly damaging".
reference: Sonora Quest Laboratories genetic report dated 4/15/2009
phenotype: Native American male from Northern AZ diagnosed at age 9. Referred for evaluation due to recurrent respiratory illness and cyclical vomiting/constipation. Other history includes- autism, developmental delay, morbid obesity (even in childhood), food/environmental allergies, asthma. Sweat testing done on two occasions with values of 68 and 64. Pancreatic sufficient. Mild lung disease. Genetic testing also showed the following polymorphisms (both of no known significance)- M470V and 2694 T>G. The PolyT status of intron 8 is 7T/7T and the TG status is 11/12

other_details: Per genetics report: The p.E1433K variant (also known as c.4297G>A) is located in exon 27 of the CFTR gene. The variant results from a G to A substitution at nucleotide position 4297. The glutamate at codon 1433 is replaced by lysine.This variant is predicted to be probably damaging by PolyPhen.
phenotype: Female born in Bangladesh and adopted/moved to the US in infancy. Hospitalized as an infant for failure to thrive which resolved. Referred for CF eval at 20yo for chronic, recurrent sinus issues including polyps and multiple sinus surgeries. Also had childhood asthma which resolved by adulthood. Pancreatic sufficient. Sweat testing done on two occasions was 63/64 and 67/76. Pt is now in her 30's and has moderate lung disease. Chronically grows staph (MSSA) on airway culture. Genetic report also identified the 13-5T variant.

c.1810A>C (p.Thr604Pro) was found along with c.1657C>T (p.Arg553* ) on the other allele in a 8y M diagnosed as CF with bronchiectasis and positive sweat chloride. He was diagnosed with nasal polyposis.
c.1367delT (p.Ala457Leufs*12) was found in a 9y F with bronchiectasis and Malnutrition. No pathogenic CFTR mutation was found on the other allele.

c.1219G>T(p.Glu407*) was found along with c.2551C>T (p.Arg851* ) on the other allele in a 3y F diagnosed as CF with bronchiectasis, sinusitis, recurrent pneumonia and malnutrition. She had meconium ileus during the neonatal period.
